title: Junior Medical Science Liaison Cardiovascular - Maternity Cover (Piemonte, Liguria, Valle d’Aosta)
description:
**Location** Basiglio, Lombardy, Italy **Job ID** R\-256440 **Date posted** 14/07/2026
  
**Introduction to role**:Are you ready to turn cardiovascular science into real\-world change across Piemonte, Liguria and Valle d’Aosta? Can you see yourself building trusted partnerships that move evidence into practice and improve outcomes for patients at scale?


In this fixed\-term role, you will be the regional scientific face for our cardiovascular portfolio, advancing a medical plan that addresses overlapping disease areas and risk factors. You will connect external expertise with internal strategy, ensuring that insights from clinical practice and real\-world data inform how we develop, communicate and deliver our science.


Working closely with colleagues across medical and commercial functions, you will lead peer\-to\-peer scientific exchange with Key External Experts and clinical investigators. Your work will accelerate evidence\-based care, support high\-quality trial execution, and equip field teams to engage healthcare professionals with clarity and credibility.


**Accountabilities**:\- Scientific Exchange and External Engagement: Act as the first regional point of contact for medical and scientific discussions on assigned products; build, expand and sustain non\-promotional peer\-to\-peer relationships with cardiologists, neurologists, internal medicine experts and other KEEs to advance the cardiovascular plan;\- Evidence Generation and Insights: Identify evidence gaps and discuss Investigator\-led research opportunities (ESR) in line with strategy and SOPs; capture, synthesize and share strategic medical insights to shape plans and pipeline decisions;\- Competitive Intelligence: Provide medical input with critical review of competitor data and materials; translate insights into timely, actionable guidance for cross\-functional partners;\- Cross\-Functional Collaboration: Work closely with field and headquarters colleagues across account management, market access, medical and analytics to uncover opportunities and deliver regional objectives through regular contacts and team meetings;\- Local Medical Planning: Identify local medical needs using an account\-centric approach; design and execute a Local Medical Plan aligned with national and global strategies and integrated into the local business plan;\- Trial Support: Partner with Medical Advisors/Medical Lead and Clinical Operations on site identification, feasibility assessment and issue resolution for national and international studies;\- Medical Education and Information: Manage medical information requests; plan and deliver compliant scientific education via face\-to\-face, virtual and regional events to disseminate current evidence to KEEs;\- Field Force Partnership: Continuously update field teams on therapeutic science and evidence to elevate engagements with medical targets, especially within priority accounts and regional scientific events;\- Society and Community Engagement: Develop and maintain collaborations with relevant scientific societies and communities to expand the reach and impact of our science;\- Compliance and Patient Safety: Conduct all activities in line with Company Values, Code of Conduct, internal procedures and Italian legislation; support the Medical and Patient Safety teams with adverse event follow\-up as required.


**Essential Skills/Experience**:\- Scientific Degree, scientific and clinical understanding;\- Fundamental knowledge of basic research, drug discovery, drug development, clinical environment;\- Knowledge of the local regulatory requirements related to medical affairs activities;\- Strong leadership capabilities;\- Empowerment and accountability;\- Excellent interpersonal skills;\- Project management experience;\- Communication skills, including communicating complex technical, scientific, or medical information;\- Ability to work in team;\- Networking and influencing skills;\- Understanding of written/spoken English;\- Willingness to be part of international teams;\- Domicile in the working area.


**Desirable Skills/Experience**:\- Knowledge of the Therapeutic Area\- Experience as MSL or Medical Affairs\- Medical Degree\- PhD in the relevant TA


**Why AstraZeneca**:Join a team where scientific rigor meets real\-world impact. Here, medical leaders and cross\-functional partners come together to generate and communicate compelling evidence that can change clinical practice—fast. You will work at the intersection of science and healthcare systems, engaging externally and internally to bring bold ideas to life. Expect unexpected teams in the same room unleashing bold thinking, the chance to see your work influence decisions across the lifecycle, and a culture that values kindness alongside ambition as we keep patients at the center of every decision.


**Call to Action**:Step into this maternity cover to shape cardiovascular care across Piemonte, Liguria and Valle d’Aosta—bring your expertise where it will change practice and accelerate your growth!


**We offer:**


Salary Range: 38\.678,40 \- 58\.017,60 EUR Annual


Contract Level: C2


CBA: Chimico\-Farmaceutico


Full\-time


Maternity Leave


**Benefits:**


**In addition to the Gross Base Salary, we offer:**


* An annual target bonus governed by the Company’s policies as amended from time to time and subject to periodic review;
* Participation Bonus (if applicable) governed by the Company’s policies as amended from time to time and subject to periodic review;
* Wellbeing benefits (welfare, insurance, psychological support, etc.) governed by the Company’s policies as amended from time to time and subject to periodic review;
* Company car;

Final compensation will be determined based on a variety of factors, including but not limited to skills, experience, internal organizational equity and other relevant factor.


**Date Posted**


15\-lug\-2026
**Closing Date**


29\-lug\-2026
AstraZeneca embraces diversity and equality of opportunity. We are committed to building an inclusive and diverse team representing all backgrounds, with as wide a range of perspectives as possible, and harnessing industry\-leading skills. We believe that the more inclusive we are, the better our work will be. We welcome and consider applications to join our team from all qualified candidates, regardless of their characteristics. We comply with all applicable laws and regulations on non\-discrimination in employment (and recruitment), as well as work authorization and employment eligibility verification requirements.

title: QA Product Validation and Approval Specialist - Medical Devices & Cosmetics Regulatory Affairs (sostituzione maternità)
Regulatory Affairs Manager - Regulatory Liaison - FSP
description:
**Work Schedule**


Standard (Mon\-Fri)**Environmental Conditions**


Office**Job Description**


At Thermo Fisher Scientific, you'll discover meaningful work that makes a positive impact on a global scale. Within our Functional Service Provider (FSP) Solutions business, we partner with leading pharmaceutical, biotechnology, and medical device organizations by providing dedicated regulatory professionals integrated within client teams.


The Regulatory Liaison serves as the primary regulatory representative supporting global clinical development programs. This role acts as the key interface between Regulatory Affairs, Clinical Operations, and other cross\-functional stakeholders to ensure timely, compliant regulatory submissions and effective execution of clinical trial activities. The position supports regulatory strategy through operational excellence while ensuring compliance with global and local regulatory requirements.

**Key Responsibilities**
========================

* Serve as the primary regulatory representative within assigned clinical trial teams.
* Coordinate and manage regulatory activities supporting global clinical development programs.
* Prepare, review, compile, and coordinate regulatory submission packages, including:


	+ Clinical Trial Applications (CTAs)
	+ Substantial Amendments
	+ Investigator's Brochures (IBs)
	+ Investigational Medicinal Product Dossiers (IMPDs)
	+ Country\-specific regulatory documentation
* Ensure submissions are completed accurately and within established timelines in accordance with applicable regional regulations, including EU Clinical Trial Regulation (CTR), ICH\-GCP, FDA, MHRA, Health Canada, and other global requirements as applicable.
* Act as the primary point of contact for regulatory operational activities between Thermo Fisher Scientific, assigned client teams, CRO partners, and regulatory vendors.
* Track regulatory milestones and submission status while maintaining oversight of regulatory deliverables.
* Maintain complete and inspection\-ready regulatory documentation throughout the clinical trial lifecycle.
* Ensure compliance with applicable regulatory requirements, client procedures, and Thermo Fisher Scientific quality standards and SOPs.
* Collaborate with Clinical Operations, Medical Affairs, Regulatory Strategy, Start\-Up, Document Management, and other cross\-functional teams to support efficient trial execution.
* Support transparency and disclosure activities where applicable.
* Identify opportunities for process improvements, operational efficiencies, and regulatory best practices.
* Provide guidance and mentorship to junior regulatory team members when appropriate.
* Participate in client governance meetings and contribute to continuous improvement initiatives within the FSP organization.

**Required Skills \& Competencies**
===================================

### **Regulatory Expertise**

* Strong understanding of global clinical trial regulations, including:


	+ EU Clinical Trial Regulation (EU CTR 536/2014\)
	+ ICH Guidelines
	+ FDA regulations
	+ Other regional regulatory frameworks
* Knowledge of clinical trial start\-up and lifecycle regulatory requirements.

### **Project Management**

* Strong organizational and planning skills.
* Ability to manage multiple studies and competing priorities.
* Demonstrated ability to work under tight timelines while maintaining high\-quality deliverables.
* Excellent attention to detail.

### **Analytical Skills**

* Ability to interpret complex regulatory requirements and translate them into operational activities.
* Strong problem\-solving and critical\-thinking abilities.
* Ability to summarize regulatory information and prepare concise documentation.

### **Technical Skills**

* Experience with:


	+ Electronic regulatory submission systems
	+ Clinical Trial Management Systems (CTMS)
	+ Electronic Document Management Systems (eTMF/EDMS)
	+ Microsoft Office Suite

### **Communication**

* Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
* Ability to build collaborative relationships across global, cross\-functional teams.
* Strong stakeholder management and influencing skills.
* Fluent English required; additional languages are advantageous.

### **Professional Attributes**

* Highly collaborative with a customer\-focused mindset.
* Demonstrates accountability and ownership.
* Adaptable within changing project environments.
* Able to work independently while contributing effectively within matrix organizations.
* Committed to quality, compliance, and continuous improvement.

**Education \& Experience**
===========================

### **Required**

* Bachelor's degree in Life Sciences, Pharmacy, Biology, Chemistry, or a related scientific discipline.


	+ Advanced degree (Master's or equivalent) preferred.
* Minimum 5 years of pharmaceutical, biotechnology, CRO, or clinical research experience.
* Minimum 3 years of experience in Regulatory Affairs supporting clinical trials.
* Experience preparing and coordinating global regulatory submissions.
* Experience working within cross\-functional global project teams.

### **Preferred**

* Experience supporting Functional Service Provider (FSP) or outsourced regulatory delivery models.
* Experience working in global, multi\-country clinical development programs.
* Knowledge of Clinical Trial Information System (CTIS).
* Experience supporting regulatory inspections or audits.

**Why Join Thermo Fisher Scientific?**
======================================

  

At Thermo Fisher Scientific, each one of our 100,000\+ extraordinary minds has a unique story to tell. Join us and contribute to our singular mission—enabling our customers to make the world healthier, cleaner and safer.


As part of our FSP Solutions team, you will:

* Work alongside leading global pharmaceutical and biotechnology organizations.
* Build expertise across diverse therapeutic areas and regulatory environments.
* Benefit from continuous learning and career development opportunities.
* Be part of an inclusive, collaborative, and innovative culture dedicated to scientific excellence.


Thermo Fisher Scientific is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive workplace where everyone can thrive

title: Territory Manager - Cardiology - Lombardia ed Emilia Romagna
description:
**Primary Purpose of the Position**


The Territory Manager is responsible for the commercial management and development of all accounts within the assigned territory. The role drives adoption of Cardiac Occluders, promotes utilization of Intracardiac Echocardiography (ICE),  

achieves sales objectives, and protects and grows market share. The position works closely with Interventional Cardiologists, Electrophysiologists, and hospital stakeholders to develop business opportunities, support procedures, and optimize customer outcomes.  

The Territory Manager serves as the primary point of contact for all commercial and technical activities within the assigned territory.


**Key Duties and Responsibilities**

**1\. Commercial Responsibilities**

* Manage all assigned accounts independently and achieve sales objectives.
* Develop and execute account plans to grow procedural volumes and market share.
* Lead commercial discussions, tenders , and purchasing processes.
* Defend Abbott pricing strategy and value proposition.
* Monitor competitive activities and identify growth opportunities.
* Maintain a strong understanding of sales performance and anticipate short\-term business results.
* Build strong relationships with key stakeholders across accounts.
* Local KOLs management coordination
* Drive adoption and business development of intra\-procedural imaging solutions (Intracardiac Echocardiography, ICE) and imaging\-guided workflows.

**2\. Technical and Clinical Responsibilities**

* Provide technical support during Cardiac Occluders procedures for left atrial appendage closure/occlusion
* Support procedural planning, case reviews and clinical discussions.
* Deliver product, therapy , and imaging education to physicians and cath lab teams.
* Act as a clinical and technical resource for imaging applications, with particular focus on Intracardiac Echocardiography (ICE).
* Support pre\-procedural planning and intra\-procedural imaging guidance.
* Collaborate with Interventional Cardiologists, Electrophysiologists and Imaging Specialists to optimize procedural workflows.
* Acting as a liaison with all cross\-functional departments within the company (Clinical Studies, Sales, Customer Service, Tender Management, etc.).

**3\. Operational Excellence**

* Maintain CRM/SFDC data and sales forecasts.
* Manage consignment inventory and product availability.
* Report Product Event Reports in accordance with company policy.
* Ensure compliance with Abbott quality, legal, and ethical standards.

**Qualifications \& Competencies**

* University degree in a scientific, biomedical, healthcare, engineering or technical discipline.
* Significant experience within the medical device industry.
* Proven experience supporting Interventional Cardiology procedures and working with Interventional Cardiologists
* Proven experience with Cardiac Occluders procedures for left atrial appendage closure/occlusion
* Proven experience supporting Interventional Cardiology procedures and working with Interventional Cardiologists.
* Experience working with Electrophysiologists and electrophysiology programs will be considered a plus
* Experience supporting Cath Lab and/or Hybrid Operating Room procedures.
* Experience with cardiac imaging workflows, including pre\-procedural planning and intra\-procedural imaging.
* Experience with Intracardiac Echocardiography (ICE) will be considered a plus
* Strong commercial, negotiation, presentation and account management skills.
* Professional proficiency in English.

title: Senior Export Manager Global Medical Device
Technical Service Engineer - EMEA
description:
**Westlake offers you the potential to enrich your work life and career experience in an entrepreneurial environment. We work together to enhance peoples' lives through our products and presence in the communities in which we operate.**

* Westlake Compounds è un fornitore leader di soluzioni avanzate a base di compound plastici per settori altamente regolamentati. Nel mercato healthcare supportiamo i nostri clienti con materiali innovativi destinati a dispositivi medici, applicazioni farmaceutiche e prodotti monouso per il settore sanitario.


Per rafforzare il nostro team tecnico presso il nostro sito di **Samarate (VA), situato in posizione strategica nell’area metropolitana di Milano e nelle immediate vicinanze dell’Aeroporto Internazionale di Milano Malpensa**, siamo alla ricerca di un **Technical Service Specialist – Healthcare Compounds EMEA .**


La persona selezionata sarà il punto di riferimento tecnico per i clienti dell’area EMEA, supportandoli nelle attività di validazione dei processi, industrializzazione dei prodotti e risoluzione di problematiche tecniche post\-vendita. Il ruolo prevede una stretta collaborazione con Sales, Product Development, Regulatory Affairs e Operations.

**Principali responsabilità**
* Fornire supporto tecnico a clienti interni ed esterni per l’ottimizzazione dei processi di trasformazione delle materie plastiche, in particolare:
	+ Stampaggio a iniezione
	+ Estrusione
	+ Soffiaggio
* Supportare i clienti durante le prove di preserie e le attività di validazione e qualificazione
* Fornire assistenza tecnica e attività di troubleshooting sia da remoto sia presso i siti dei clienti
* Preparare e gestire la documentazione tecnica necessaria a supportare i processi di qualificazione e conformità normativa
* Organizzare ed erogare corsi di formazione, workshop e seminari tecnici
* Effettuare benchmark e analisi comparative dei prodotti Westlake destinati al settore healthcare
* Collaborare con il team commerciale fornendo supporto tecnico durante lo sviluppo di progetti e opportunità di business
* Contribuire al mantenimento e allo sviluppo delle relazioni con i clienti
* Collaborare con le funzioni interne coinvolte, tra cui Product Development, Material Development, Regulatory Affairs e Operations
* Partecipare ad ulteriori attività e progetti tecnici in funzione delle esigenze aziendali
* **Requisiti**
* Laurea in Scienza dei Materiali, Ingegneria, Chimica o discipline tecniche affini **oppure** diploma tecnico accompagnato da una significativa esperienza nella lavorazione delle materie plastiche
* Gradita esperienza professionale di circa 2–3 anni in ambito Technical Service, Application Support, trasformazione delle materie plastiche o ruoli tecnici analoghi
* Esperienza pratica nella lavorazione delle materie plastiche, con particolare focus sullo stampaggio a iniezione
* La conoscenza dei processi di estrusione e/o soffiaggio costituisce un vantaggio
* Esperienza con compound in PVC o conoscenza delle formulazioni di compound plastici rappresenta un plus
* Conoscenza dei principi di validazione e qualificazione dei processi
* Esperienza nell’assistenza tecnica e nella risoluzione di problematiche di processo (troubleshooting)
* Esperienza nella conduzione di revisioni tecniche, workshop o seminari
* Esperienza in settori altamente regolamentati; la conoscenza delle normative relative ai dispositivi medici costituisce un requisito preferenziale
* Ottime capacità comunicative, relazionali e di lavoro in team
* Approccio orientato al cliente, autonomia operativa e capacità di problem solving
* Buona conoscenza della lingua inglese, scritta e parlata
* Disponibilità a trasferte nell’area EMEA fino al 30% del tempo lavorativo
* **Cosa offriamo**


Entrando in Westlake, Lei entrerà a far parte di un’organizzazione internazionale che valorizza qualità, sicurezza, innovazione e sviluppo continuo delle persone.
* Retribuzione annua lorda 40\.000\-50\.0000 €
* Sistema di bonus basato sulle performance complessive dell’organizzazione
* Buoni pasto del valore di € 8,00 giornalieri
* Programmi di formazione continua su competenze tecniche e trasversali
* Ambiente di lavoro internazionale e collaborativo
* Elevato livello di autonomia e responsabilità
* Coinvolgimento in progetti innovativi per il settore healthcare e medical
* **Come candidarsi**


Se desidera mettere a frutto la Sua esperienza tecnica in un contesto internazionale e contribuire allo sviluppo di soluzioni innovative per il settore healthcare, saremo lieti di ricevere la Sua candidatura.

**Si prega di inviare il curriculum vitae e gli eventuali documenti di candidatura in lingua italiana ed inglese, poiché il processo di selezione prevede il coinvolgimento di stakeholder internazionali.**


Westlake is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to any characteristics protected by applicable legislation.

**If you are an active Westlake employee (or an employee of any Westlake affiliates), please do not apply here. You will apply via the Jobs Hub application in Workday.**

title: Policy & Advocacy Manager (m/f/d)
description:
**Policy \& Advocacy Manager (m/f/d)**

  


  

  

Bayer S.p.A. is looking for a dynamic and strategic **Policy \& Advocacy Manager.** He/she will contribute to the development and implementation of Policy \& Advocacy activities at the national level, supporting the company's institutional positioning and advocacy initiatives aimed at facilitating access to innovation and promoting the sustainability of the healthcare system, in alignment with the Market Access strategy.

  


**YOUR TASKS AND RESPONSIBILITIES:**


* Support the definition and implementation of Policy \& Advocacy priorities in line with portfolio and access needs.
* Monitor developments in the regulatory and policy environment (AIFA, Ministry of Health, Parliament, Regions), identifying implications and potential impacts for the company.
* Contribute to the development of policy content and key messages on strategic topics such as innovation, HTA, regional access, and National Health Service (NHS) sustainability.
* Manage and develop operational relationships with national and regional institutional stakeholders.
* Participate in institutional meetings, technical working groups, and advisory boards in coordination with the MIART Head.
* Support engagement with patient associations, scientific societies, and industry associations as part of advocacy initiatives.
* Draft policy briefs, position papers, legislative analyses, and institutional documentation.
* Support the preparation of contributions for public consultations, hearings, and advocacy initiatives.
* Collect and analyze clinical, economic, and healthcare system evidence to support policy activities.
* Manage Policy \& Advocacy projects at both national and regional levels, including educational initiatives, policy forums, and public\-private partnerships.
* Collaborate in the development of multi\-stakeholder initiatives focused on areas of shared interest.
* Ensure alignment of initiatives with access priorities and the company’s institutional objectives.
* Work closely with Value Access, Medical Affairs, Communications, Commercial, and the Nucleus Community teams.
* Provide institutional and regulatory insights to support access and launch strategies.
* Prepare updates, presentations, and reports for national and, where required, international management.

  


**WHO YOU ARE:**


* University degree in Political Science, Law, Economics, Healthcare, Public Health, or a related field.
* Master's degree or specialized training in Public Policy, Market Access, or Health Economics is preferred.
* Minimum of 3 years of experience in Policy \& Advocacy, Government Affairs, or Market Access.
* Strong understanding of the Italian healthcare system and key decision\-making processes.
* Experience in managing institutional stakeholders and coordinating advocacy initiatives.
* Solid knowledge of national and regional institutional dynamics (Ministry of Health, AIFA, Regional Authorities).
* Strong policy writing and synthesis capabilities.
* Excellent organizational and project management skills.
* Fluent English.
* Proactive mindset with a solution\-oriented approach.
* Strong institutional communication skills, both written and verbal.
* Ability to work effectively in complex, multi\-stakeholder environments.
* Reliability, attention to detail, and results orientation.

  



To attract and retain our talent at Bayer, we are committed to paying compensation based on gender\-neutral criteria and in any case on objective, fair and performance\-based criteria.The initial annual gross compensation for this role is not below è 51\.300,00 EUR. Any upward adjustments may be made in the presence of objective circumstances, such as experience, specific qualifications, and/or specific areas of responsibility, and in any case based on gender\-neutral criteria.

  



Type of Contract: Permanent



Place of work: Milano

title: Product Management
description:
Product Management OLG
  
  

WHO WE ARE
  
  

POLYVANTIS is one of the leading global suppliers in the market for high\-quality acrylic and polycarbonate films and sheets. Formed by combining two iconic brands in the industry \- PLEXIGLAS® for polymethylmethacrylate semi\-finished products and LEXAN™ for polycarbonate sheets and films. With 1,500 employees and 15 production sites worldwide, we offer unmatched global capabilities, providing customers with innovative solutions in sheets and films. Our products are used in various applications including stadium roofing, facade glazing, LED lighting, signage, machine guards, motorcycle windshields, train seats, aircraft interiors, security \& ID cards, bullet\-resistant glazing and many more.
  
  

WHAT YOU WILL DO
  
  

* Regional product management management of the profitability and growth of portfolio in one or more plants.
* P\&L, profitability \& product positioning responsibility for specific POLYVANTIS product portfolio in EUR (Approximately $70MM in revenue).
* Develop long\-term product portfolio strategy and together with Manufacturing, develop long\-term asset strategy and investment in line with business strategy.
* Contribute to product positioning in focus market segments through articulation of the product portfolio value propositions and lead programs for strategic cost out.
* Identify strategic co\-producer/collaboration opportunities in the value chain of product line upstream and downstream and track execution.
* Build relationships with key customers for key focus segments. Play a critical role in defining long\-term developmental contracts \& programs with such accounts.
* Understand global and regional competitive landscape and incorporate in product portfolio strategy to win.
* Drive execution for critical new product programs in close cooperation with Technology \& Innovation and Marketing Communications, to ensure timely and effective product launches.
* Keeping product testing and technical manuals current, follow regulatory \& compliance trends to proactively initiate changes needed to the product line.
* Establish the pricing floors with the strategic pricing team.


WHAT WE ARE SEEKING
  
  

**First of all, your personality:** you are characterized by a high level of motivation, enjoyment of work and a conscientious way of working.
  
  

You have a master degree in the field of chemical engineering, polymer engineering or materials science
  
  

You have a work experience of minimum 3 years Product Management experience in the chemical or plastics markets. Technical experience in the plastics industry is a clear plus.
  
  

Working experience in an intercultural environment is required. Affinity \& experience with the Construction, Automotive, Lighting, Medical and Home Appliances lndustry preferred.
  
  

You have a very good knowledge of English, additional languages are a plus
  
  

You have user knowledge of common MS Office and CRM products (SAP preferred)
  
  

WHAT WE OFFER
  
  

**We listen:** Your ideas along our corporate values of creativity, openness, responsibility and entrepreneurial spirit are important to us.
  
  

**Remuneration:** Attractive salary in the chemical tariff (Gross Annual Salary range € 80\.000\-90\.000, the salary will be adequate to the experience and capability proven by the candidate), participation in the company's success
  
  

**Coverage:** Medical insurance, group accident insurance
  
  

**Work\-life\-balance:** Flexible working, remote working. The position is open in Italy but we consider also candidates from other EU Country
  
  

**Health and social affairs:** Prevention programs, meal allowance
  
  

...and much more. You can find an overview of our benefits on our careers page.
  
  

YOUR APPLICATION
  
  

**Sounds interesting? Learn more about us and apply here:** https://www.polyvantis.com/en/career . Realize your dreams at POLYVANTIS!
  
  

You don't meet all the requirements? No problem \- for us, it's not just about certificates, professional qualifications and previous experience, but also about personal development potential.
  
  

POLYVANTIS seeks the best candidates, and as a result is an equal opportunity employer that is proud to provide equal access to jobs regardless of protected status.

title: Key Accounts Manager
description:
* Milan, Lombardy, Italy
* Remote Available
* Sales
* REF25043D


  
### **Company Description**


It started with a simple idea: what if surgery could be less invasive and recovery less painful? Nearly 30 years later, that question still fuels everything we do at **Intuitive**. As a global leader in **robotic\-assisted surgery** and **minimally invasive care**, our technologies—like the **da Vinci surgical system** and **Ion**—have transformed how care is delivered for millions of patients worldwide.


We’re a team of engineers, clinicians, and innovators united by one purpose: to make surgery smarter, safer, and more human. Every day, our work helps care teams perform with greater precision and patients recover faster, improving outcomes around the world.


The problems we solve demand creativity, rigor, and collaboration. The work is challenging, but deeply meaningful—because every improvement we make has the potential to change a life.


If you’re ready to contribute to something bigger than yourself and help **transform the future of healthcare**, you’ll find your purpose here.


### **Job Description**


The da Vinci Key Accounts Manager plays a critical role in building and developing the installed base of da Vinci Surgical Systems within IDNs. The major responsibilities are to diagnose and provide training, sales and marketing plan in order to sell, develop and manage champions throughout a health system. This includes surgeons, hospital executives, finance, physician and corporate executives. The objective is to grow both the clinical and capital business across a health system. This responsibility will require an individual able to master and teach all aspects of the clinical and capital sales process, and be able to work throughout all levels of the company.


**Roles and Responsibilities:**


* Diagnose, build plans and execute plans in order to sell system within the listed accounts in working with Sales Directors
* Develop and sell advanced clinical pathways to enable surgeons to adopt da Vinci and improve patient outcomes
* Manage and follow up with customers on regional pricing, program and capital initiatives
* Oversee local and regional level academic training of residents in targeted accounts within IDNs in working with clinical, marketing and training team
* Collaborate with market access, Government affairs and other key Ecosystem support team members to expand access of da Vinci technology at local and regional levels
* Conduct clinical and economic value analysis (QTIs) in working with surgeons and hospitals
* Develop and execute regional commercial strategy at local and country levels
* Create and execute market\-wide strategies for all specialties
* Make executive presentations at local and corporate level
* Eventually teach all aspects of clinical and capital sales process
* Negotiate local and group sales
* Manage the development and implementation of local, regional and national sales campaigns
* Develop and expand key surgeons/customer relationships
* Plan weekly and quarterly sales activities in support of sales goals
* Travel extensively to customer sites (2\-4 days weekly with 50\+% travel) and company meetings

### **Qualifications**


**Required Knowledge, Skills, and Experience:**


* 7\+ Medical sales experience
* Knowledge of the major regional and national healthcare groups
* Excellent communication and interpersonal skills
* Clinical and Capital medical equipment sales experience
* Experience in selling new technologies and in Sales Management

  



### **Additional Information**


Intuitive is an Equal Employment Opportunity Employer. We provide equal employment opportunities to all qualified applicants and employees, and prohibit discrimination and harassment of any type, without regard to race, sex, pregnancy, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, color, age, religion, protected veteran or disability status, genetic information or any other status protected under federal, state, or local applicable laws.


We will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and conviction records in accordance with fair chance laws.


U.S. Export Controls Disclaimer: In accordance with the U.S. Export Administration Regulations (15 CFR §743\.13(b)), some roles at Intuitive Surgical may be subject to U.S. export controls for prospective employees who are nationals from countries currently on embargo or sanctions status.


Certain information you provide as part of the application will be used for purposes of determining whether Intuitive Surgical will need to (i) obtain an export license from the U.S. Government on your behalf (note: the government’s licensing process can take 3 to 6\+ months) or (ii) implement a Technology Control Plan (“TCP”) (note: typically adds 2 weeks to the hiring process).


For any Intuitive role subject to export controls, final offers are contingent upon obtaining an approved export license and/or an executed TCP prior to the prospective employee’s start date, which may or may not be flexible, and within a timeframe that does not unreasonably impede the hiring need. If applicable, candidates will be notified and instructed on any requirements for these purposes.


This position may be filled at a different job level than listed here depending on
  

business need and/or on the selected candidate’s experience, knowledge and skills.

title: Manager, PV (Medical Project Coordinator Team), EMEA
description:

Standard (Mon\-Fri)**Environmental Conditions**


Office**Job Description**


Join us as Manager PVG – And lead the efforts in managing and optimizing drug safety operations to ensure the highest standards of patient safety and regulatory compliance in clinical trials.  

  

What You’ll Do:  

* Manage multiple aspects of projects, both local and globally, such as contractual, procedural and regulatory requirements.
* Maintain good communications with other functional groups concerning program and company issues. Collaborating with other employees in problem


solving and systems improvement.  

* Review regulatory/pharmacovigilance publications and information sources to keep updated on current regulations, practices, procedures and proposals.
* Maintain a basic medical understanding of applicable therapeutic areas and disease states. May participate in on\-call duties for specific projects and ensure 24\-hr coverage for intake of cases from investigative sites.
* Provide plans and accurate forecasts for all projects.
* Serve as a resource for other departmental managers and lead departmental initiatives.
* Prepare and present proposals.
* Manage staff of experienced professionals, which may include interviewing and selection, job description preparation, professional development, goal setting, performance management, coaching and mentoring, employee counseling, and separations. Approve courses of action on salary administration, hiring, corrective action, and terminations. Review and approve time records, expense reports, requests for leave, and overtime.

  

Education and Experience:  

* Bachelor's degree or equivalent and relevant formal academic/vocational qualification to include 3\+ years of safety experience
* Previous experience to provide the knowledge, skills, and abilities to perform the job (comparable to 5\+ years).
* 1\+ year of leadership responsibility

  

In some cases, an equivalency, consisting of a combination of appropriate education, training and/or directly related experience, will be considered sufficient for an individual to meet the requirements of the role.  

  

Knowledge, Skills and Abilities:  

* Manages experienced professionals and / or subordinate management who exercise latitude and independence in their assignments. Often heads one or more sections or a small department.
* Working knowledge of the global regulatory requirements and the importance of and compliance with procedural documents
* Strong knowledge of relevant therapeutic areas as required for processing AEs
* General knowledge of pathophysiology and the disease process
* Time management and project management skills
* Proficiency in Microsoft Office products and safety databases
* Good command of English and ability to translate information into local language where required
* Excellent oral and written communication skills
* Strong attention to detail and accuracy
* Strong critical thinking, problem solving and decision\-making skills

  

Working Conditions and Environment:  

* Work is performed in an office environment with exposure to electrical office equipment
* Daily exposure to high pressure and intense concentration
* Daily interactions with clients/associates
* Long, varied hours on occasion
* Travel required occasionally domestic and/or international

  

Physical Requirements:  

* Frequently vertical and /or stationary for 6\-8 hours per day.
* Repetitive hand movement of both hands with the ability to make fast, simple, repeated movements of the fingers, hands, and wrists.
* Frequent mobility required.
* Frequent bending and twisting of upper body and neck.
* Light to moderate lifting and carrying (or otherwise moves) objects including luggage and laptop computer with a maximum lift of 15\-20 lbs.
* Ability to access and use a variety of computer software developed both in\-house and off\-the\-shelf.
* Ability to communicate moderately complex information and ideas so others will understand; with the ability to listen to and understand information and ideas presented through spoken words and sentences.
* Frequently interacting with others, relates sensitive information to diverse groups.
* Ability to apply basic principles to solve conceptual issues.
* Performs a wide range of variable tasks as dictated by variable demands and changing conditions with little predictability as to the occurrence. Ability to perform under stress. Ability to multi\-task.
* Regular and consistent attendance.

  

Why Join Us?  

When you join Thermo Fisher Scientific, you become part of a global team that values passion, innovation, and a commitment to scientific excellence. You’ll work in an environment where collaboration and development are part of the everyday experience – and where your contributions truly make a difference.  

  

Apply today to help us deliver tomorrow’s breakthrough.

title: Robotics Engineer
description:
**Join the Future of Regenerative Medicine**
--------------------------------------------


**Multiply Labs** is a robotics company on a mission to solve a significant bottleneck in modern medicine: **the manufacturing of life\-saving cell therapies.**



Currently, cell therapies are produced through manual, labor\-intensive processes. This keeps costs astronomical—often exceeding **$1M per patient**—and limits access to only a fraction of those in need. We are revolutionizing this process by building the world’s first modular autonomous robotic factories for pharmaceutical production.


### **Why This Matters**



We don’t just build robots; we build the infrastructure for the next generation of medicine. Our technology allows biopharma companies to scale production without redesigning their science, drastically lowering regulatory hurdles and bringing costs down to a level where these treatments can actually reach patients.


### **The Stack \& The Scale**


* **The Mission:** Build the world’s best robots to make life\-saving therapies accessible to all.
* **The Technology:** Advanced robotic orchestration, high\-precision automation, and cloud\-integrated hardware\-software systems.
* **The Location:** Headquartered in San Francisco, CA.
* **The Backing:** Supported by premier investors, including **Casdin Capital, Lux Capital, and Y Combinator.**

### **About The Role**



We are looking for a hands\-on Robotic Engineer who is passionate about applied technology and thrives in an operational environment. You will join our integration and testing team, working directly on the robotic cells that automate cell therapy manufacturing in a GMP setting. You will own the full cycle — from device configuration through full\-cell qualification — and play a key role in bringing our systems from design to deployment.


### **Key Responsibilities**


* Configure and test electromechanical devices in preparation for full robotic cell qualification
* Collaborate with electricians and assembly teams to diagnose and resolve issues during configuration and testing phases
* Execute test protocols using internal software, ensuring full traceability and quality of results
* Manage the organization and documentation of tested devices and materials
* Support the software integration of new devices and peripherals
* Actively participate in all stages of robotic cell qualification, from setup through final execution
### **Required Qualifications**


* **Programming:** Advanced proficiency in Python
* **Operating systems:** Linux and Windows; confident use of the terminal and development tools
* **Version control:** Git (daily use in a team environment)
* **Robotics:** hands\-on experience with industrial or collaborative robots, or a strong theoretical foundation in kinematics, dynamics, and control
* **Hands\-on mindset:** strong manual dexterity and ability to work physically with complex electromechanical systems
* **Problem\-solving:** empirical approach, comfort with experimentation, and ability to find solutions in the field
* **Technical reading:** ability to read electrical schematics, mechanical drawings, and product documentation
* **Organization:** autonomy, proactivity, and ability to manage priorities in a fast\-paced environment
* **Communication:** ability to clearly communicate technical ideas and schedule risks to distributed, international teams
* **Languages:** English B2\+ (written and spoken for technical documentation and US team communication); Italian C2 (Native/Bilingual proficiency).
* **Mobility:** valid driver's license (A/B), own vehicle required

#### ***Additional Preferred Experiences:***



* MSc in Mechanical Engineering, Mechatronics, Robotics, or related field
* PhD in Robotics or Autonomous Systems
* Experience in regulated environments (GMP, ISO 13485, or equivalent)
* Familiarity with ROS or control frameworks for collaborative robots
### **Perks \& Benefits**


### **Total Rewards \& Life at Multiply Labs**


* **Competitive Compensation:** Salary range of **€50,000\.00 \- €70,000** plus **meaningful early\-stage equity.** We want our team to have a real stake in the future we’re building.
* **Premium Health:** Premium Medical, Dental, and Vision plans with **subsidies for child dependents.**
* **Wealth Building:** 401(k) with a **4% company match** to help you plan for the long term.
* **Pre\-Tax Commuter Benefit.** Pay for eligible transit and parking with pre\-tax dollars to save on commuting costs.
* **Parental Support:** Robust **Paid Parental Leave** to support your family’s newest additions.
* **True Flexibility:** We offer **Flexible PTO** and **12 Paid Holidays**, trusting you to manage your time and recharge when you need it.
* **No Set Hours:** Set your own schedule in alignment with your manager and team. We hold a high bar, measuring success by impact and outcomes – not hours logged.
* **Office Perks:** A vibrant office culture featuring **catered team lunches**, stocked kitchen, and frequent team events.
* **H\-1B and Green Card sponsorship** available for exceptional talent.
* **Referral bonuses** for helping us build a world\-class team.
* **Great people, meaningful work**, and a team that genuinely **believes in what we're building**.

Come build the world’s best robots—and help make life\-saving therapies accessible to all.



We actively seek candidates with diverse perspectives and lived experiences. We’re committed to building a team where everyone feels valued, respected, and empowered to do their best work.



Multiply Labs encourages candidates from all backgrounds to apply — even if you don’t meet every qualification. If you’re excited about our mission and think you can contribute, we’d love to hear from you.



We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: Instruments Calibration Manager
description:
Date: 21 Jul 2026
Department: Plant Operations Italy, Nerviano Site
Business Area: Industrial Operations \& HSE
Job Type: Direct Employee
Contract Type: Permanent
Location:
Milano, IT


***About us***


Chiesi is an international research\-focused biopharmaceutical group with 90 years’ experience, operating in more than 30 countries, with its Headquarter in Parma, Italy. More than 8,000 employees across the group are united by a singular purpose: promoting a healthier world for our people, patients, and the planet. This is what drives us as we research, develop, and market innovative drugs across our main therapeutic areas. Discover more here.


At Chiesi we are thinking generations ahead, driving sustainable innovation with purpose. This fosters a culture of reliability, transparency, and ethical behaviour at every level. As a Benefit Corporation and a certified B Corp, we have embedded sustainability in our bylaws and continuously measure our impact.


Diversity and inclusion are at the heart of who we are. We believe our differences make us stronger. We are a vibrant ecosystem of passionate, talented individuals united by strong values, each bringing unique perspectives that help us to continuously challenge the status quo for the better.

  

***Global Technical Operations \& Supply***


Global Technical Operations \& Supply (Global TechOp\&S) is Chiesi’s global manufacturing and supply area, responsible for ensuring consistent, outstanding quality, service and value across our end‑to‑end supply chain. Global TechOp\&S oversees the entire lifecycle of the drug: from process optimization and industrialization, to manufacturing, engineering, quality oversight, and supply chain management. Throughout this journey, Operational Excellence drives continuous improvement. This end‑to‑end model is powered by our four manufacturing sites \- Parma (IT), Blois (FR), Santana de Parnaíba (BR), and Nerviano (IT) \- together with our Virtual Plant network of external partners.


**This is what you will do**
----------------------------


As a Calibration Manager, you will lead the calibration program for both Utilities and Process Equipment across the manufacturing site, ensuring compliance with GMP requirements and company quality standards. Working closely with engineering, manufacturing, QA, Validation, and Maintenance teams, you will ensure that calibration activities are planned, executed, documented, and continuously improved.


**You will be responsible for**
-------------------------------


* Managing the site calibration program for Utilities and Process Equipment.
* Ensuring all calibration activities are performed in compliance with GMP, regulatory requirements, and internal procedures.
* Developing and maintaining the calibration master plan and ensuring timely execution of scheduled activities.
* Managing calibration records, certificates, and documentation to ensure data integrity and inspection readiness.
* Coordinating internal resources and external service providers performing calibration activities.
* Supporting qualification, validation, commissioning, and start\-up activities for new equipment and utilities.
* Defining calibration strategies, intervals, tolerances, and acceptance criteria based on risk assessment and regulatory expectations.
* Managing deviations, investigations, change controls, and CAPAs related to calibration systems.
* Ensuring the calibration status of critical instruments used in manufacturing, utilities, laboratories, and packaging areas.
* Monitoring KPIs related to calibration performance and implementing continuous improvement initiatives.
* Supporting internal and external audits as the subject matter expert for calibration processes.
* Leading and developing the calibration team while promoting a culture of safety, quality, and operational excellence.
**You will need to have**
-------------------------


* Degree in Engineering or a related scientific discipline or technical diploma
* Minimum 5 years of experience in calibration, maintenance, engineering, or technical services within the pharmaceutical or other GMP\-regulated industry.
* Strong knowledge of calibration principles for process instrumentation and utility systems.
* Experience with GMP regulations, Data Integrity principles, and pharmaceutical quality systems.
* Knowledge of utilities such as HVAC, Purified Water, Water for Injection (WFI), Clean Steam, Compressed Air
* Experience with process equipment including filling lines, packaging equipment, CIP/SIP systems, and manufacturing equipment.
* Familiarity with CMMS and calibration management systems.
* Experience managing contractors and external calibration service providers.
* Experience supporting regulatory inspections by FDA, AIFA
* Excellent knowledge of written and spoken English
**Location**
------------


Nerviano, Milan


**Compensation Range**
----------------------


The minimum salary for this role is € 52\.000, aligned to our established salary framework. The final offer will reflect the candidate’s skills, experience, qualifications and internal equity considerations.


This role is eligible to participate in an annual bonus scheme, subject to company and individual performance.


In addition, we offer a comprehensive benefits package including pension, private medical insurance, wellbeing programme, flexible benefits programme.


**What we offer**


No matter where your path starts at Chiesi, it leads to inspiring possibilities. Your future is our focus, and we are committed to nurturing your development in our dynamic, friendly environment with access to resources and training every step of the way.


We provide top\-class benefits, including comprehensive healthcare programs, work\-life balance initiatives, and robust relocation support. Our salary package is competitive, comprising a basic salary, performance bonuses, and benefits benchmarked against the external market. Additionally, we offer flexible working arrangements, remote work options, and tax assistance services for foreign colleagues, all designed to help you thrive.

  

*Chiesi is an equal opportunity employer committed to hiring a diverse workforce at all levels of our business. All qualified applicants receive consideration for employment based on attitude and merit, without regard to race, national origin, age, sex, religion, disability, sexual orientation, marital status, military status, gender identity or expression, or any other basis protected by applicable laws. This policy applies to all aspects of employment, including hiring, transfer, promotion, compensation, eligibility for benefits, and termination.*

title: Senior Inspector / Expediter - Rotating Equipment - EI & Packages
description:
Wood is currently recruiting for a **Senior Inspector / Expediter** for rotating equipment skid mounted, machineries and packages (e.g. centrifugal and reciprocating compressors, steam and gas turbines, centrifugal pumps, centrifugal fans, gearboxes, bridge cranes and hoists, loading arms, HVAC systems, etc.). The position will be based in Corsico (Milan) office, with the possibility to work from home up to two days per week.


**\#LI\-Hybrid**

**The Role**

In the execution of his day\-by\-day job the Inspector / Expediter shall carry out the project specific inspections and release the materials as detailed in the supplier’s Inspection and Test Plan (ITP) and as directed and instructed by the home office Inspection \& Expediting Coordinator. The Inspector / Expediter shall conduct: Witness, Hold, Monitor and Review activities as per the approved Supplier’s / Sub\-supplier’s ITP.


**Our Clients and Projects**

**Designing the future. Transforming the world.**

You will be working for major international clients in the energy market and material market, and you will be part of a team results focused that will provide technical solutions.


**What we can offer**

* **Meaningful and interesting projects** delivered to leaders of industry across chemical, petrochemical, oil\&gas and emerging energy sectors
* **Flexible working arrangements** that balance client, team and individual needs offering hybrid working where relevant
* **Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion** ; we are an organisation actively committed to diversity and inclusion across our business
* **Compensation** – details provided at the end of the job advert
* **Benefits package** ; Free shuttle bus from Metro station to the office, company canteen, biannual free medical check\-up, free company parking, extra\-working accidents insurance
* **Commitment to continued professional development;** development plans that are tailored to your individual needs and interests
* **Global connections** ; join experts around the world who are at the leading edge of our industry, shaping the standards of our profession

  
**The key objectives for this role are:**

Duties specifically related to the function of Inspector are the following:


* Perform Vendor evaluation for qualification and inclusion in the Wood Global Vendor List
* Perform Vendor QC system audits and surveillances
* Be familiar with the material requisition requirements and Wood / client project specifications and drawings
* Review of supplier’s ITP on the base of the requirements set forth in the purchase order, material requisition, job specifications, codes, standards, statutory regulations, etc
* Review of supplier’s inspection and testing procedures on the base of the requirements set forth in the purchase order, material requisition, job specifications, client specifications, codes, standards, statutory regulations, etc
* Attend and lead pre\-inspection meetings / progress review meetings at supplier’s workshop.
* Witness of shop inspection and testing of material and equipment as detailed in the supplier’s ITP
* Prepare and issue the Inspection Reports after each inspection visit
* Issue Vendor Non\-Conformity Reports (VNCR) and expedite their close out
* Check the compliance of materials and equipment with purchase order requirements
* Ensure that all works undertaken are completed in accordance with the project specifications
* Be fully aware of the concessions raised, approved and / or rejected by the requisitioning Engineer
* Review and acceptance of supplier final inspection data books and material certificates to release the materials
* Prepare and issue the final inspection reports
* Liaise with the requisitioning Engineer and Inspection \& Expediting Coordinator as appropriate
* To report promptly to the Inspection \& Expediting Coordinator all matters related to the inspections and testing attended
* Provide feedback on supplier / sub\-supplier performance upon request
* Review and analysis of supplier’s / sub\-supplier’s fabrication schedules
* Review and analysis of the progress status of all supplier’s / sub\-supplier’s activities, i.e. engineering, procurement of material, fabrication, inspection \& testing, packing and shipment.
* Issue of expediting reports after each visit, highlighting critical areas that require recovery or corrective actions
* Expediting of corrective actions required to resolve fabrication or delivery problems

Duties specifically related to the function of shop Expediter are the following:


* Review and analysis of supplier’s / sub\-supplier’s fabrication schedules
* Review and analysis of the progress status of all supplier’s / sub\-supplier’s activities, i.e. engineering, procurement of material, fabrication, inspection \& testing, packing and shipment.
* Issue of expediting reports after each visit, highlighting critical areas that require recovery or corrective actions
* Expediting of corrective actions required to resolve fabrication or delivery problems

  
At Wood, we are committed to equal opportunities and welcome all talented individuals to consider joining our team. So even if you don't match every statement below but feel you have some of the experience, knowledge or skills needed for this role, we encourage you to apply. It will take all of us working together to deliver solutions to the world’s most critical challenges.


**Expected:**

* University Degree or Technical Diploma
* At least 10 years of experience in the field of inspection and testing of rotating equipment skid mounted, machineries and packages
* Certified Level 2 Operator for non\-destructive testing (PT, MT, UT, RT) in accordance with the rules established by the standard UNI EN ISO 9712, or trained and certified according to other international standards equivalent to UNI EN ISO 9712 (e.g. ASNT SNT\-TC\-1A)
* Working knowledge of the following standards: API 610, API 611, API 612, API 613, API 616, API 617, API 618, API 673, API 681
* Has a broad knowledge of construction, fabrication and inspection techniques
* Has knowledge of quality management systems including ISO 9001
* Good planning and organizational skills
* Has a pro\-active approach in resolving any Quality Control issues / action
* Maintains a professional, safe and ethical standard of behavior in the relationship with Suppliers, Client representatives, or third parties
* knowledge of MS office
* Fluent English and Italian
* Speaks and writes clearly and informatively
* Takes responsibility for subordinates’ activities, follows instructions, and responds to the Manager of Inspection \& Expediting
* Follows Company policies and procedures
* Completes inspection and expediting reports as well as the review of Supplier’s ITP and inspection and testing procedures in timely manner
* Self\-motivated
* Committed to identify, analyze, and propose solutions to problems in a constructive and positive manner
* Availability to move to the job site is essential

**Compensation and compliance:**

**Contract \& Classification**

* Contract type: Permanent
* Collective Agreement (CCNL): Industria Metalmeccanica (CNEL C011\)
* Job Classification level: quadro, consistent with role requirements and applicable CCNL

**Compensation**

* Gross Annual Salary: the initial gross annual salary will be € 58,000, paid in 14 instalments
* The compensation package will include applicable CCNL elements and company benefits
* Variable pay and additional benefits will be detailed during the selection process

**Equal Opportunities**

* This job opportunity is open to all individuals, in compliance with the principle of equal opportunities and non\-discrimination, in accordance with applicable regulations

**Transparency \& Offer Determination**

At the end of the selection process, the Company reserves the right to make an offer with a job level and/or gross annual salary different from what is indicated in this job posting, consistently with the candidate’s professional experience and technical and soft skills.


Any deviation from the information provided in the job posting will be justified by objective and verifiable elements that emerged during the selection process, including:


* Professional experience significantly higher/lower than the target profile;
* Additional specialized skills relevant to the role;
* Scope and complexity of responsibilities assigned in the final job configuration;
* Alignment with the internal grading system and consistency with comparable roles;
* Outcomes of technical evaluations and/or assessments used in the selection process

**Compliance Statement**

**All compensation decisions:**

* Comply with the principle of equal pay for equal work or work of equal value pursuant to EU Directive 2023/970 and applicable Italian legislation – D.L. 96/2026
* Are aligned with the relevant CCNL applicable
* Follow company Code of Conduct and DE\&I principles

  
Wood is a global leader in consulting, engineering and operations for the energy and materials sectors. With 33,000 people in around 50 countries, Wood supports clients across the full asset lifecycle, delivering safe, predictable outcomes while enabling resilient operations and a lower carbon future. Wood forms the Energy \& Materials pillar of Sidara \- a global partnership uniting leading multidisciplinary engineering, design, and project management companies. www.woodgroup.com

All suitably qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment on the basis of objective criteria and without regard to the following (which is a non\-exhaustive list): race, colour, age, religion, gender, national origin, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity, protected veteran status, or other characteristics in accordance with the relevant governing laws.

description:

title: Quality Analyst
description:
**Position Summary**
--------------------



The Quality Analyst is responsible for assisting in administration of the quality management system at a site to assure adherence to ISO 9001, ISO 13485, FDA 21 CFR Part 820/211, EU GMPs, ISO 17025, ISO 11137 and/or ISO 11135 and other applicable regulatory standards. This role leads complaint/CAPA investigations, supplier quality improvement initiatives, continuous improvement initiatives and countermeasures with the use of statistical techniques and other accepted quality principles. This role executes activities concerned with the development, implementation, maintenance, and continuous improvement of STERIS quality systems. The Analyst also supports production and quality operations by coordinating and performing problem\-solving investigations, pre\-reviews and final acceptance/run release of processing runs that contain non\-conformances.  

Responsible for the activities related to quality and regulatory compliance to ensure all product processing requirements are met in accordance with Customer specifications including calibrations, dosimetry results, generation of test methods, audits, scheduling, validations, record keeping and reporting.

**Please be aware that this is a temporary maternity cover position.****Duties**
----------



1\. Coordinate day\-to\-day tasks and direction for Quality Technicians/Senior Quality Technicians by providing technical support, mentoring and oversight.


2\. Responsible for oversight of all product and/or report pre\-reviews and releases conducted by Quality Technicians/Senior Quality Technicians.


3\. Responsible for executing product and/or report releases for all processing runs/lab samples containing a non\-conformance, including management of Customer communication and leading the associated non\-conformance investigations.


4\. Initiate and/or participate in corrective actions, problem\-solving and continuous improvement activities.


5\. Perform internal audits at other AST facilities, including identification of non\-compliances and report generation. Responsible for managing remediation of internal audit observations assigned by site Quality Manager.


6\. Manage creation of new quality system policies and procedures and revisions to existing policies/procedures.


7\. Review collected data to perform statistical analysis and recommend process changes to improve quality.


8\. Monitor and report on performance metrics.


9\. Instruct other STERIS employees in quality principles, effective corrective actions, and valid statistical techniques.


10\. Collaborate with other departments and facilities within the company on quality related issues.


11\. Lead quality system programs (i.e. calibration, maintenance, training, CAPA, complaints, non\-conformances, supplier quality, management review, operational qualifications, document control, change control, risk management, etc).


12\. Overall responsibilities include commitment to ensure external and internal requirements ar met according to documented policies, procedures, standards and regulations.


13\. Perform other duties as assigned.

**Education Degree**
--------------------


* Bachelor's Degree
**Required Experience**
-----------------------


* 1\-5 years of combined Manufacturing/Quality Engineering and/or Quality Systems experience.
* 1\-5 years of experience with medical device or other regulated industries preferred.
* 1\-5 years of experience working in an ISO certified environment required.
* Working knowledge of FDA QSR/ EUGMP regulations strongly preferred.
* Excellent problem\-solving skills
* Focus on identification of potential issues and continuous improvement.
* Experience working on cross\-functional teams and on own initiative.
* Demonstrated excellent organizational, oral and written communications skills.
* Must have PC experience, as well as working familiarity of desktop applications including Excel, Word, and PowerPoint.
* Ability to work in a fast\-paced, regulated environment with strict deadlines.

description:
**Work Schedule**


Standard (Mon\-Fri)**Environmental Conditions**


Office**Job Description**


Join Us as a Manager Medical Writing – Make an Impact at the Forefront of Innovation  

We have successfully supported the top 50 pharmaceutical companies and more than 750 biotechs, spanning 2,700 clinical trials across 100\+ countries in the last 5 years.  

  

As part of our expert team, you’ll have the opportunity to ensure operational excellence that makes a real difference in organizational performance. As a Manager Medical Writing, you will manage the performance and workload of Medical Writers and provide short\-term and long\-term resource planning to assign projects to appropriate and available staff. You will supervise the preparation of Medical Writing deliverables and organize processes for the completion of projects within established timelines. You will evaluate the timeliness and quality of deliverables. You will assist writers in the presentation and interpretation of data and perform senior reviews to ensure overall adherence to departmental standards. Additionally, you will participate in the preparation and review of group SOPs and WPDs, evaluating compliance with SOP/WPD on an ongoing basis. You will give marketing presentations to potential clients on the capabilities of the Medical Writing department and interact with Bids and Contracts and Business Development to prepare budgets and proposals for Medical Writing projects. You will also participate in the development of training programs for Medical Writing and ensure direct reports are adequately trained.  

  

What You’ll Do:  

* Supervises Medical Writers. Provide short\-term and long\-term resource planning in order to assign projects to appropriate and available staff. Holds regularly scheduled meetings with staff and provide updates to senior management. Review and approve semimonthly time sheets. Assist staff to forecast project\-related costs and timelines for completion. Hires and evaluates employees. Recommends salary levels and promotions
* Supervises the preparation of Medical Writing deliverables. Organizes processes for completion of projects within established timelines. Evaluate timeliness and quality of deliverables and provides updates to senior management. Acts as resource for questions relating to study design, adverse event reporting, and statistical analyses. Assists writers in presentation and interpretation of data. Performs senior review to ensure overall adherence to departmental standards. Write and review group SOP and WPD. Evaluates compliance with SOP/WPD on an ongoing basis. Stays updated on relevant publication guidelines and requirements (GPP, ICMJE, etc.)
* Gives marketing presentations to potential clients on the capabilities of the Medical Writing department. Interacts with Bids and Contracts and Business Development to prepare proposals for Medical Writing projects.
* Develops and maintains training programs for Medical Writing. Ensures that all writers are adequately trained.
* Uses scientific knowledge to assist in the written interpretation of scientific data and to guide others in their presentation of scientific data.

  

Education and Experience Requirements:  

* Bachelor’s degree in a science\-related field required; Advanced degree (PharmD, PhD, Masters) preferred
* Previous experience that provides the knowledge, skills, and abilities to perform the job (comparable to 8\+ years) or an equivalent combination of education, training, and experience. Minimum of 5 years of relevant experience in medical publications writing, preferably within the pharmaceutical, biotechnology, CRO, or medical communications industry. Demonstrated experience managing scientific writing staff, overseeing publication deliverables, and supporting publication planning and execution is required."
* 1\+ year of leadership responsibility


Years of experience refers to typical years of related experience needed to gain the required knowledge, skills, and abilities necessary to perform the essential functions of the job. Years of experience are not to be used as the only determining factor in establishing the job class or making employment selection decisions.  

  

Knowledge, Skills and Abilities:  

* Ability to supervise, evaluate, and mentor departmental personnel
* Ability to oversee assignments, monitor staff workload, and provide guidance for completion of assignments
* Ability to create and modify project timelines, allocate resources, and forecast departmental workload
* In depth knowledge of departmental processes and ability to contribute to improving and developing processes
* Excellent medical writing skills, including excellent grammatical, editorial, and proofreading skills, and the ability to effectively interpret and present extremely complex data
* Effective communication, negotiation, and presentation skills needed to represent the department in a range of situations including bid defenses, capabilities presentations, and audits
* Ability to develop, review, and modify proposals and budgets for presentation to clients

  

Why Join Us?  

  

When you join Thermo Fisher Scientific, you become part of a global team that values passion, innovation, and a commitment to scientific excellence. You’ll work in an environment where collaboration and development are part of the everyday experience—and where your contributions truly make a difference.  

  

Apply today to help us deliver tomorrow’s breakthroughs.
